lessons.title: What is your name?
lessons.topic: Identity and the sounds of F and G

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you ask if you want to know a new friend's name?
questions.hint: It's a question you use when meeting someone for the first time.

questions.question_text: How do you greet a friend when you meet them?
questions.hint: It's something you say when you start talking to someone.

questions.question_text: If someone asks, 'What is your name?', what do you say?
questions.hint: You tell them something personal about you.

steps.title: Funny Goat
steps.text: Hello! My name is Anna. I am a girl. This is my goat. He is a funny goat. I have a fish too.

steps.explanation: Use 'This is my' to show what you have.

steps.audio_text: Hi! I'm Jake. This is my fish. He is happy and swims a lot.

steps.prompt: Say: My name is [Your Name] and I have a goat.
steps.expected_text: my name is [your name] and i have a goat

questions.question_text: I ___ Ben. (Grammar question)
Which is an animal? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: A goat can swim.
What is the boy's name in the story? (Reading comprehension)

lessons.title: He and She
lessons.topic: Gender pronouns and the sound of H

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Is the person in the video a boy or a girl?
questions.hint: Think about what boys are called.

questions.question_text: What sound do you hear at the start of 'he'?
What word can describe a girl?
questions.hint: Starts with the same sound as 'shoe'.

steps.title: The Park
steps.text: My name is Tom. He is Jack. He is my friend. She is Lily. She has a red hat. This is my favorite park.

steps.explanation: Use 'He' for boys and 'She' for girls.

steps.audio_text: This is Lisa. She is a girl. She has a pink hat.

questions.question_text: Fill in the blank: He ___ a hat. (Grammar question)
What word matches the emoji 👒? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: She is a girl means the person is a boy.
Who is she? She is ___. (Reading comprehension question)
What is the first sound in 'hat'? (Phonics question)

lessons.title: Meet My Friend
lessons.topic: Introduction of others and sounds of I and J

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What sound does the 🎨 brush make when it starts?
What is the first sound in the word ‘juice’?
Which letter sounds like the start of ‘ice cream’?
questions.hint: It's the sound at the start of 'igloo'.

steps.title: Leo and the Jet
steps.text: This is my friend. His name is Leo. He is happy in an igloo! Look at the jet! It flies fast.

steps.explanation: Use 'He is my' to introduce a boy.

steps.audio_text: Hello, this is my friend Jack! He is smiling because he is flying a jet.

questions.question_text: This is my ___ igloo. (Vocabulary question)
Which word starts with the same sound as 'igloo'? (Phonics question)
What does 'This is my friend' mean? (Reading comprehension)
True or False: A jet can fly.
He is ___ happy. (Grammar question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you wear on your head that starts with 'H'?
questions.hint: You put it on your head to keep it warm.

questions.question_text: Which animal name starts with 'F'?
Which picture shows something that jumps and starts with 'F'?
questions.hint: It likes to be near water and hops.

steps.title: My Name is Ben
steps.text: Hello! My name is Ben. I am a boy. This is Lily. She is my friend. We see a frog and a cat.

questions.question: What do Ben and Lily see?

steps.audio_text: Hi there! My name is Ben, and I have a sister named Lisa. She loves playing with dolls.

steps.prompt: Say: My name is Emma and this is my friend Jack.
steps.expected_text: my name is emma and this is my friend jack

questions.question_text: I ___ Ben. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'teacher'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Hello' is a greeting.
What comes after 'F' in the alphabet? (Reading comprehension question)
This is my ____. He is my brother. (Grammar question)
